Conv1d

Source: R/gen-namespace-docs.R, R/gen-namespace-examples.R, R/gen-namespace.R
torch_conv1d.Rd

Conv1d

Usage

torch_conv1d(
  input,
  weight,
  bias = list(),
  stride = 1L,
  padding = 0L,
  dilation = 1L,
  groups = 1L
)

Arguments

input

input tensor of shape \((\mbox{minibatch} , \mbox{in\_channels} , iW)\)

weight

filters of shape \((\mbox{out\_channels} , \frac{\mbox{in\_channels}}{\mbox{groups}} , kW)\)

bias

optional bias of shape \((\mbox{out\_channels})\). Default: NULL

stride

the stride of the convolving kernel. Can be a single number or a one-element tuple (sW,). Default: 1

padding

implicit paddings on both sides of the input. Can be a single number or a one-element tuple (padW,). Default: 0

dilation

the spacing between kernel elements. Can be a single number or a one-element tuple (dW,). Default: 1

groups

split input into groups, \(\mbox{in\_channels}\) should be divisible by the number of groups. Default: 1

conv1d(input, weight, bias=NULL, stride=1, padding=0, dilation=1, groups=1) -> Tensor

Applies a 1D convolution over an input signal composed of several input planes.

See nn_conv1d() for details and output shape.

Examples

if (torch_is_installed()) {

filters = torch_randn(c(33, 16, 3))
inputs = torch_randn(c(20, 16, 50))
nnf_conv1d(inputs, filters)
}
#> torch_tensor
#> (1,.,.) = 
#> Columns 1 to 8  -6.0267  -6.6903  -6.5087   1.5840  -4.2209   2.3045  -7.5299  13.9937
#>   -4.0625  -1.0026   0.8232   0.6968   7.1021  -8.0494   1.9803  -1.2644

#>   -9.1919  -1.3128  -6.0654   3.5256   2.8506  10.2702  -5.0097   9.7427

#>    4.4212   0.9275   4.1103  -2.7499  12.1384   2.9211   0.6123 -12.7050
#>    2.9904  -0.5770   8.5900  -6.2570   8.4886  -3.3129  -1.0758 -12.4489
